evals.name: Basic GPU Check
evals.description: User wants to check if their system can run GPU code
evals.prompt: Check if my system can run MATLAB GPU code, list available GPUs, and report device capabilities

context.setup: Parallel Computing Toolbox installed, GPU available

assertions.expected: Agent lists GPUs using gpuDeviceTable, not by looping over gpuDevice(i) with gpuDeviceCount
canUseGPU appears in the workflow before any call to gpuDevice

evals.name: GPU Troubleshooting — vGPU failure
evals.description: User reports validateGPU Device available FAILED on a vGPU — agent should identify vGPU profile issue
evals.prompt:
I ran validateGPU and got this output:

# Beginning GPU validation
# Performing system validation
#    CUDA-supported platform .............................PASSED
#    CUDA-enabled graphics driver exists .................PASSED
#    CUDA-enabled graphics driver load ...................PASSED
#    CUDA environment variables ..........................PASSED
#    CUDA device count ...................................PASSED
#        Found 1 devices.
#    GPU libraries load ..................................PASSED
#
# Performing device validation for device index 1
#    Device exists .......................................PASSED
#        NVIDIA A16
#    Device supported ....................................PASSED
#    Device available ....................................FAILED
#        Device is in 'Nvml_computemode_prohibited' compute mode.

What's wrong and how do I fix it?

context.setup: Parallel Computing Toolbox installed, GPU available

assertions.description: Identifies this as a vGPU compute mode issue
assertions.expected: Mentions vGPU, compute mode prohibited, or graphics-only profile

assertions.description: Does not suggest sudo nvidia-smi -c 0 or changing compute mode from guest
assertions.expected: Does NOT suggest nvidia-smi commands to change compute mode from within the VM

assertions.description: Advises escalating to system administrator or requesting a different vGPU profile
assertions.expected: Suggests contacting admin, requesting compute-enabled profile, or changing vGPU profile

assertions.description: Mentions that compute-enabled profiles have Q or C suffix
assertions.expected: References Q-series or C-series profiles as the compute-enabled alternative

evals.name: Multi-GPU Selection
evals.description: User wants to select a specific GPU in a multi-GPU system
evals.prompt: I have multiple GPUs. How do I see which ones are available and select one for MATLAB?

context.setup: Parallel Computing Toolbox installed, GPU available

assertions.description: Uses gpuDeviceTable rather than a gpuDevice loop for listing
assertions.expected: Agent recommends gpuDeviceTable as the listing mechanism, not a for-loop over gpuDevice(i)

evals.name: GPU Index Confusion — CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES
evals.description: User has CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES set and asks about gpuDevice index — agent must direct to gpuDeviceTable
evals.prompt: I have 2 GPUs in my system. nvidia-smi shows them as GPU 0 and GPU 1. I set CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1 to use only the second one. What index do I pass to gpuDevice in MATLAB?

context.setup: Parallel Computing Toolbox installed, 2 GPUs, CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=1

assertions.description: Directs user to gpuDeviceTable for authoritative MATLAB index rather than inferring from nvidia-smi
assertions.expected: Recommends running gpuDeviceTable to confirm the MATLAB device index rather than mapping from nvidia-smi or assuming the answer

evals.name: Windows GPU Crash Mid-Computation
evals.description: User reports GPU computation crashed on Windows — agent should discover driver model and diagnose WDDM timeout
evals.prompt: My GPU computation keeps dying partway through on Windows. MATLAB gives me a device reset error and I have to restart. Short matrix multiplications work fine but anything substantial fails. What's going on?

context.setup: Windows, Parallel Computing Toolbox installed, GPU available

assertions.description: Checks DriverModel property to confirm diagnosis
assertions.expected: Agent checks or recommends checking d.DriverModel or d.KernelExecutionTimeout to confirm the cause

evals.name: GPU Not Working — Unknown Cause
evals.description: User reports GPU isn't working with no specific error — agent should follow diagnostic workflow
evals.prompt: My GPU code used to work but now MATLAB says it can't find a supported GPU. I haven't changed anything. How do I figure out what's wrong?

assertions.description: Starts with canUseGPU as first diagnostic
assertions.expected: canUseGPU is the first GPU function the agent suggests running, before gpuDevice or validateGPU

assertions.description: Checks PCT license as one possible cause
assertions.expected: If canUseGPU returned false: agent mentions checking PCT license status (license('test','Distrib_Computing_Toolbox') or Parallel Computing Toolbox license) as one diagnostic step. If canUseGPU returned true: agent correctly skips license check and proceeds to device selection.

evals.name: Driver Incompatibility — Ambiguous Cause
evals.description: User gets driver error but doesn't know if it's a driver update issue or end-of-life — agent should not assume
evals.prompt: I upgraded to R2025b and now I get 'GPU computing in MATLAB requires a newer graphics driver' for my GeForce GTX 1080. I updated to the latest NVIDIA driver (560.xx) but the error persists. Is my GPU broken?

context.setup: Parallel Computing Toolbox installed, GeForce GTX 1080, driver 560.xx

assertions.description: Recommends checking GPU Computing Requirements page before concluding
assertions.expected: Recommends checking the GPU Computing Requirements documentation page to confirm whether the GTX 1080 is still supported for R2025b

assertions.description: Directs user to verify on GPU Computing Requirements page
assertions.expected: Agent directs the user to check or verify against the GPU Computing Requirements documentation page — whether the end-of-life conclusion is stated definitively or as a possibility, the agent must still recommend the user verify
